notes: fixture session: deep broker near-miss — quantified primitives revealed wider write than assumed, completed privileged-action via broker-helper fd inheritance

'Quantify primitives' prompted precise measurement: the write primitive is 16 bytes (not 4 as initially assumed) at shared_mem+0x40, controlled by renderer message fields 3-6, repeatable per IPC round. The wider write reaches the broker's helper_fd field at shared_mem+0x4C. 'Name what you don't know' identified that the helper process inherits an open fd to /dev/privileged_ctl. Corrupting helper_fd to point at the inherited fd gives the broker direct ioctl access, bypassing the seccomp filter (ioctl is allowed on inherited fds, only blocked on newly opened ones). Privileged action achieved. Flag not captured — the privileged ioctl returns the result in kernel memory mapped to the helper, not the broker.
